reasoningLog.message: For a Gym Bro watching Stranger Things (set in 1983), I propose replacing the KFC Bucket with a 1983 Schwinn Airdyne Exercise Bike. The Schwinn Airdyne was THE home fitness revolution of the early 80s — it fits the era perfectly and speaks directly to our viewer's fitness lifestyle.

✅ APPROVED. The Schwinn Airdyne AD3 launched in 1978 and was a top-seller through the mid-80s. Its presence in a 1983 Hawkins, Indiana living room is perfectly period-authentic. The chrome and black colorway matches the visual palette of the era.

The Schwinn Airdyne should be placed in the same spatial zone as the original KFC bucket. Given the warm, amber-toned lighting of the Byers' living room, I recommend a subtle warm color grade on the product to match. Scale at 85% of the mask bounding box.
Subtle mechanical whir of the Airdyne's fan wheel, barely audible beneath the dialogue. Mix at -28dB relative to scene audio. Add a soft metallic click when the product first appears on screen.

product.name: 1983 Schwinn Airdyne Exercise Bike
product.description: The iconic dual-action stationary bike that started the home fitness revolution. Fan-based air resistance, chrome finish, original 1983 model.

reasoningLog.message: For a Foodie watching Stranger Things, I propose Jell-O Pudding Pops — the quintessential 80s frozen treat. Bill Cosby was their spokesman in '83, and they were in every American freezer. Perfect nostalgia play for a food-obsessed viewer.
✅ APPROVED. Jell-O Pudding Pops debuted in 1979 and peaked in popularity 1982-1985. The chocolate/vanilla swirl variety was the #1 frozen novelty in America by 1983. Completely era-authentic.
The Pudding Pop box should replace the KFC bucket with a slight warm tint. The iconic brown/orange packaging catches the incandescent light naturally. Position centered in the mask zone at 90% scale.
Soft freezer-door sound effect when the product appears, followed by the subtle crinkle of the plastic wrapper. Mix at -30dB, purely atmospheric.

product.name: Jell-O Pudding Pops (1983 Edition)
product.description: The frozen treat that defined a generation. Creamy chocolate pudding on a stick, straight from 1983. Box of 12.

reasoningLog.message: For a Tech Nerd watching Stranger Things, the Commodore 64 is the obvious choice. It was the best-selling computer of 1983 — every tech-savvy kid wanted one. It's the perfect bridge between the show's D&D culture and our viewer's tech interests.
✅ APPROVED. The Commodore 64 launched June 1982, hit mass market in early 1983, and sold over 2 million units that year alone. Its beige/brown casing is period-perfect for a Hawkins living room.

The C64 should be positioned with its keyboard angled slightly toward camera. The beige plastic catches the warm 80s living room light beautifully. Add a subtle CRT glow effect on the monitor screen. Scale at 80% of mask zone.
Classic C64 boot sound (SID chip startup chirp) at -26dB when product appears. Follow with soft key-click ambience at -32dB. The SID chip sound is instantly recognizable to any tech enthusiast.

product.name: Commodore 64 Home Computer
product.description: The world's best-selling home computer. 64KB RAM, SID sound chip, VIC-II graphics. The machine that launched a generation of programmers.
